bead.title: code-health: deduplicate rate-limit-retry policy shared by agent and agent/try (try reimplements it)
bead.description:
PROBLEM/ROOT CAUSE: cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go reimplements, body-identical, code already in cli/internal/agent/ratelimit_retry.go: ParseRetryAfter (ratelimit_retry.go:190 vs try/attempt.go:594), ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr (:227 vs :618), IsRateLimit*/IsRateLimitReport/IsRateLimitResult (:129 vs :553), rateLimitBackoffSchedule (:37 vs :434, 5 identical entries), and types RateLimitWaitDecision/RateLimitRetryInfo/RateLimitRetryConfig + consts RateLimitRetryDefaultBudget/PerWaitCap/BudgetExhaustedReason. Cause: try imports only internal/bead, not internal/agent, so it cloned rather than imported.

PROPOSED FIX: Create a leaf package internal/ratelimitpolicy holding the schedule, the three types, the consts, and the pure parse/classify funcs. Both internal/agent and internal/agent/try import it; keep the Report/Result-typed wrappers (IsRateLimitReport vs IsRateLimitResult) as thin adapters in each caller.

NON-SCOPE: no change to retry budgets/schedule values. Behavior-preserving.
bead.acceptance:
1. rateLimitBackoffSchedule defined exactly once.
2. try/ no longer defines ParseRetryAfter/ExtractRetryAfterFromStderr (grep 0 in try/).
3. Existing rate-limit tests in internal/agent AND internal/agent/try pass unchanged.
4. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/agent/try/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
